<span class="cline-any cline-neutral">&nbsp;</span></td><td class="text"><pre class="prettyprint lang-js">import { Request, Response, NextFunction } from "express";
import { checkTenantAccess } from "../services/tenantAccess.service";
import { query } from "../lib/db";
&nbsp;
type ReqWithJwt = Request &amp; {
  jwt?: {
    sub: string;
    role?: string;
  };
};
&nbsp;
export function requireTenantAccess(
  action: string,
  allowedRoles: string[] = []
) {
  return async function (
    req: ReqWithJwt,
    res: Response,
    next: NextFunction
  ) {
    try {
      if (!req.jwt?.sub) {
        return res.status(401).json({
          error: "JWT missing",
        });
      }
&nbsp;
      const identityId = req.jwt.sub;
      const tenantId = req.params.tenantId;
&nbsp;
      if (!tenantId) {
        return res.status(400).json({
          error: "tenantId required",
        });
      }
&nbsp;
      // âœ… Keto relation check
      const allowed = await checkTenantAccess(
        tenantId,
        identityId,
        action
      );
&nbsp;
      if (!allowed) {
        return res.status(403).json({
          error: "Tenant access denied",
        });
      }
&nbsp;
      // âœ… Optional role check (DB)
      if (<span class="missing-if-branch" title="if path not taken" >I</span>allowedRoles.length &gt; 0) {
        const roleRow = await query(
          `SELECT role FROM tenant_users
           WHERE tenant_id=$1 AND identity_id=$2`,
          [tenantId, identityId]
        );
&nbsp;
        <span class="missing-if-branch" title="if path not taken" >I</span>if (!roleRow.rows.length) {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >          return res.status(403).json({</span>
            error: "No tenant role mapping",
          });
        }
&nbsp;
        const role = roleRow.rows[0].role;
&nbsp;
        if (!allowedRoles.includes(role)) {
          return res.status(403).json({
            error: "Role not permitted",
          });
        }
      }
&nbsp;
      next();
    } catch (err) {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      console.error("Tenant guard failed:", err);</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      res.status(500).json({</span>
        error: "Tenant authorization failed",
      });
    }
  };
}
&nbsp;</pre></td></tr></table></pre>

// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >import { logger, RouteRegistrator } from "../pkg"</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >import { UserErrorCard } from "@ory/elements-markup"</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >import { format } from "@redtea/format-axios-error"</span>
import { NextFunction, Request, Response } from "express"
&nbsp;
function <span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >formatRequest(</span>req: Request) {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  return {</span>
    headers: req.headers,
    method: req.method,
    url: req.url,
    httpVersion: req.httpVersion,
    body: req.body,
    cookies: "[Redacted]",
    path: req.path,
    protocol: req.protocol,
    query: req.query,
    hostname: req.hostname,
    ip: req.ip,
    originalUrl: req.originalUrl,
    params: req.params,
  }
}
&nbsp;
export const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >register500Route: RouteRegistrator = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >(</span>a</span>pp, createHelpers) =&gt; {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  app.use(<span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >(e</span>rr: Error, req: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction) =&gt; {</span>
    let jsonError: string = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >""</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    try {</span>
      const formattedError = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >format(err)</span>
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      logger.error("An error occurred", {</span>
        error: formattedError,
        req: formatRequest(req),
      })
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      delete (formattedError as any).config</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      delete (formattedError as any).isAxiosError</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      jsonError = JSON.stringify(formattedError)</span>
    } catch (e) {
      // This shouldn't happen, as the try block should not throw an error.
      // But if it does, we log it and render the Error card with a generic error message.
      // If this is removed, the server will crash if the error is not serializable.
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      logger.error("An error occurred while serializing the error", {</span>
        error: err,
        req: formatRequest(req),
      })
    }
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    res.status(500).render("error", {</span>
      card: UserErrorCard({
        title: "Internal Server Error",
        cardImage: createHelpers?.(req, res).logoUrl,
        backUrl: req.header("Referer") || "welcome",
        error: {
          id: "interal_server_error",
          error: {
            message:
              "An internal server error occurred. Please try again later.",
            debug: JSON.parse(jsonError),
          },
        },
      }),
    })
  })
}
&nbsp;</pre></td></tr></table></pre>

<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >import {</span>
  defaultConfig,
  logger,
  RouteCreator,
  RouteRegistrator,
  setSession,
} from "../pkg"
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >import { oidcConformityMaybeFakeSession } from "./stub/oidc-cert"</span>
import { AcceptOAuth2ConsentRequestSession } from "@ory/client"
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >import { UserConsentCard } from "@ory/elements-markup"</span>
import { Request, Response, NextFunction } from "express"
&nbsp;
const extractSession = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >(</span></span>
  req: Request,
  grantScope: string[],
): AcceptOAuth2ConsentRequestSession =&gt; {
  const session: AcceptOAuth2ConsentRequestSession = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >{</span>
    access_token: {},
    id_token: {},
  }
&nbsp;
  const identity = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >req.session?.identity</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  if (!identity) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    return session</span>
  }
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  if (grantScope.includes("email")) {</span>
    const addresses = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >identity.verifiable_addresses || []</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    if (addresses.length &gt; 0) {</span>
      const address = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >addresses[0]</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      if (address.via === "email") {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        session.id_token.email = address.value</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        session.id_token.email_verified = address.verified</span>
      }
    }
  }
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  if (grantScope.includes("profile")) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    if (identity.traits.username) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      session.id_token.preferred_username = identity.traits.username</span>
    }
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    if (identity.traits.website) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      session.id_token.website = identity.traits.website</span>
    }
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    if (typeof identity.traits.name === "object") {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      if (identity.traits.name.first) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        session.id_token.given_name = identity.traits.name.first</span>
      }
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      if (identity.traits.name.last) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        session.id_token.family_name = identity.traits.name.last</span>
      }
    } else <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >if (typeof identity.traits.name === "string") {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      session.id_token.name = identity.traits.name</span>
    }
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    if (identity.updated_at) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      session.id_token.updated_at = parseInt(</span>
        (Date.parse(identity.updated_at) / 1000).toFixed(0),
      )
    }
  }
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  return session</span>
}
&nbsp;
// A simple express handler that shows the Hydra consent screen.
export const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >createConsentRoute: RouteCreator =</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >  (c</span>reateHelpers) =&gt;</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >  async (r</span>eq: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >) =&gt; {</span></span></span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    res.locals.projectName = "Consent"</span>
&nbsp;
    const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >{</span>
      oauth2,
      isOAuthConsentRouteEnabled,
      shouldSkipConsent: canSkipConsent,
      logoUrl,
    } = createHelpers(req, res)
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    if (!isOAuthConsentRouteEnabled()) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      res.redirect("404")</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      return</span>
    }
&nbsp;
    const { <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >consent_challenge } = r</span>eq.query
&nbsp;
    // The challenge is used to fetch information about the consent request from ORY hydraAdmin.
    const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >challenge = String(consent_challenge)</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    if (!challenge) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      next(</span>
        new Error("Expected a consent challenge to be set but received none."),
      )
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      return</span>
    }
&nbsp;
    // This section processes consent requests and either shows the consent UI or
    // accepts the consent request right away if the user has given consent to this
    // app before
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    await oauth2</span>
      .getOAuth2ConsentRequest({ consentChallenge: challenge })
      // This will be called if the HTTP request was successful
      .then(<span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >async ({</span> data: body<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" > })</span> =&gt; {
        // If a user has granted this application the requested scope, hydra will tell us to not show the UI.
        if (<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >canSkipConsent(</span>body)) {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >          logger.debug("Skipping consent request and accepting it.")</span>
          let <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >grantScope = body.requested_scope || []</span>
          const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >session = extractSession(req, grantScope)</span>
&nbsp;
          // Now it's time to grant the consent request. You could also deny the request if something went terribly wrong
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >          await oauth2</span>
            .acceptOAuth2ConsentRequest({
              consentChallenge: challenge,
              acceptOAuth2ConsentRequest: {
                // We can grant all scopes that have been requested - hydra already checked for us that no additional scopes
                // are requested accidentally.
                grant_scope: grantScope,
&nbsp;
                // ORY Hydra checks if requested audiences are allowed by the client, so we can simply echo this.
                grant_access_token_audience:
                  body.requested_access_token_audience,
&nbsp;
                // The session allows us to set session data for id and access tokens
                session,
              },
            })
            .then(<span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >({</span> data: body<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" > })</span> =&gt; {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >              logger.debug("Consent request successfuly accepted")</span>
              // All we need to do now is to redirect the user back to hydra!
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >              res.redirect(String(body.redirect_to))</span>
            })
            .catch(next)
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >          return</span>
        }
&nbsp;
        // this should never happen
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        if (!req.csrfToken) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >          logger.warn(</span>
            "Expected CSRF token middleware to be set but received none.",
          )
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >          next(</span>
            new Error(
              "Expected CSRF token middleware to be set but received none.",
            ),
          )
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >          return</span>
        }
&nbsp;
        // If consent can't be skipped we MUST show the consent UI.
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        res.render("consent", {</span>
          card: UserConsentCard({
            consent: body,
            csrfToken: req.csrfToken(true),
            cardImage: body.client?.logo_uri || logoUrl,
            client_name:
              body.client?.client_name ||
              body.client?.client_id ||
              "Unknown Client",
            requested_scope: body.requested_scope || [],
            client: body.client,
            action: "consent",
          }),
        })
      })
      // This will handle any error that happens when making HTTP calls to hydra
      .catch(next)
    // The consent request has now either been accepted automatically or rendered.
  }
&nbsp;
export const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >createConsentPostRoute: RouteCreator =</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >  (c</span>reateHelpers) =&gt; <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >async (r</span></span>eq, res, next<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >) =&gt; {</span></span></span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    res.locals.projectName = "Consent"</span>
    // The challenge is a hidden input field, so we have to retrieve it from the request body
    const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >{ oauth2, isOAuthConsentRouteEnabled } = c</span>reateHelpers(req, res)
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    if (!isOAuthConsentRouteEnabled()) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      res.redirect("404")</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      return</span>
    }
&nbsp;
    const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >{</span>
      consent_challenge: challenge,
      consent_action,
      remember,
      grant_scope,
    } = req.body
&nbsp;
    let <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >grantScope = grant_scope</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    if (!Array.isArray(grantScope)) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      grantScope = [grantScope]</span>
    }
    // extractSession only gets the sesseion data from the request
    // You can extract more data from the Ory Identities admin API
    const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >session = extractSession(req, grantScope)</span>
&nbsp;
    // Let's fetch the consent request again to be able to set `grantAccessTokenAudience` properly.
    // Let's see if the user decided to accept or reject the consent request..
    if (<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >consent_action </span>=== "accept") {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      logger.debug("Consent request was accepted by the user")</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      await oauth2</span>
        .getOAuth2ConsentRequest({
          consentChallenge: String(challenge),
        })
        .then(<span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >async ({</span> data: body<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" > })</span> =&gt; {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >          return oauth2</span>
            .acceptOAuth2ConsentRequest({
              consentChallenge: String(challenge),
              acceptOAuth2ConsentRequest: {
                // We can grant all scopes that have been requested - hydra already checked for us that no additional scopes
                // are requested accidentally.
                grant_scope: grantScope,
&nbsp;
                // If the environment variable CONFORMITY_FAKE_CLAIMS is set we are assuming that
                // the app is built for the automated OpenID Connect Conformity Test Suite. You
                // can peak inside the code for some ideas, but be aware that all data is fake
                // and this only exists to fake a login system which works in accordance to OpenID Connect.
                //
                // If that variable is not set, the session will be used as-is.
                session: oidcConformityMaybeFakeSession(grantScope, session),
&nbsp;
                // ORY Hydra checks if requested audiences are allowed by the client, so we can simply echo this.
                grant_access_token_audience:
                  body.requested_access_token_audience,
&nbsp;
                // This tells hydra to remember this consent request and allow the same client to request the same
                // scopes from the same user, without showing the UI, in the future.
                remember: Boolean(remember),
&nbsp;
                // When this "remember" sesion expires, in seconds. Set this to 0 so it will never expire.
                remember_for: process.env.REMEMBER_CONSENT_SESSION_FOR_SECONDS
                  ? Number(process.env.REMEMBER_CONSENT_SESSION_FOR_SECONDS)
                  : 3600,
              },
            })
            .then(<span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >({</span> data: body<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" > })</span> =&gt; {
              const redirectTo = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >String(body.redirect_to)</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >              logger.debug("Consent request successfuly accepted", redirectTo)</span>
              // All we need to do now is to redirect the user back!
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >              res.redirect(redirectTo)</span>
            })
        })
        .catch(next)
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      return</span>
    }
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    logger.debug("Consent request denied by the user")</span>
&nbsp;
    // Looks like the consent request was denied by the user
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    await oauth2</span>
      .rejectOAuth2ConsentRequest({
        consentChallenge: String(challenge),
        rejectOAuth2Request: {
          error: "access_denied",
          error_description: "The resource owner denied the request",
        },
      })
      .then(<span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >({</span> data: body<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" > })</span> =&gt; {
        // All we need to do now is to redirect the browser back to hydra!
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        res.redirect(String(body.redirect_to))</span>
      })
      // This will handle any error that happens when making HTTP calls to hydra
      .catch(next)
  }
&nbsp;
export const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >registerConsentRoute: RouteRegistrator = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >(</span></span></span>
  app,
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  createHelpers = defaultConfig,</span>
) =&gt; {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  app.get(</span>
    "/consent",
    setSession(createHelpers),
    createConsentRoute(createHelpers),
  )
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  app.post(</span>
    "/consent",
    setSession(createHelpers),
    createConsentPostRoute(createHelpers),
  )
}
&nbsp;</pre></td></tr></table></pre>

&nbsp;
type OAuth2Error = {
  error: string
  error_description?: string
  error_hint?: string
}
&nbsp;
function <span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >isOAuth2Error(</span>query: qs.ParsedQs): query is OAuth2Error {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  return query.error !== undefined</span>
}
&nbsp;
/**
 * Returns an error object, either from Ory Identities or an OAuth2 error.
 *
 * @param frontend the frontend SDK
 * @param query the query parameters as received from the request
 * @returns a FlowError object
 */
async function <span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >fetchError(</span>
  frontend: FrontendApi,
  query: qs.ParsedQs,
): Promise&lt;FlowError&gt; {
  // If the error is an OAuth2 error, its details are encoded into the URL.
  // We can simply decode them and return them here.
  if (<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >isOAuth2Error(</span>query)) {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    return {</span>
      id: decodeURIComponent(query.error.toString()),
      error: {
        status: "OAuth2 Error",
        id: decodeURIComponent(query.error.toString()),
        message: decodeURIComponent(
          query.error_description?.toString() || "No description provided",
        ),
        ...(query.error_hint
          ? { hint: decodeURIComponent(query.error_hint.toString()) }
          : {}),
        code: 599, // Dummy code to trigger the full error screen
      },
    }
  } else if (<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >isQuerySet(</span>query.id)) {
    // If the error comes from Ory Identities/Kratos, we need to fetch its details from the backend.
    // Once that's done, we can return the error.
    const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >res = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >await frontend.getFlowError({ id: query.id })</span></span>
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    if (res.status !== 200) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      throw new Error("No error was found")</span>
    }
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    return res.data</span>
  }
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="branch-0 cbranch-no" title="branch not covered" >  throw new Error("No error was found")</span></span>
}
&nbsp;
// A simple express handler that shows the error screen.
export const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >createErrorRoute: RouteCreator =</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >  (c</span>reateHelpers) =&gt; <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >async (r</span></span>eq, res<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >) =&gt; {</span></span></span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    res.locals.projectName = "An error occurred"</span>
&nbsp;
    // Get the SDK
    const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >{ frontend, logoUrl } = c</span>reateHelpers(req, res)
&nbsp;
    try {
      const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >error = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >await fetchError(frontend, req.query)</span></span>
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      res.status(200).render("error", {</span>
        card: UserErrorCard({
          error,
          cardImage: logoUrl,
          backUrl: req.header("Referer") || "welcome",
        }),
      })
    } catch (err) {
      let <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >error: FlowError</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      if (isAxiosError(err)) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        error = err.response?.data.error</span>
      } else {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        error = {</span>
          id: "Failed to fetch error details",
          error: err as GenericError,
        }
      }
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      res.status(200).render("error", {</span>
        card: UserErrorCard({
          error: {
            id: "Failed to fetch error details",
            error: {
              ...error,
              code: 500,
            },
          },
          cardImage: logoUrl,
          title: "An error occurred",
          backUrl: req.header("Referer") || "welcome",
        }),
      })
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      return</span>
    }
  }
&nbsp;
export const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >registerErrorRoute: RouteRegistrator = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >(</span></span></span>
  app,
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  createHelpers = defaultConfig,</span>
) =&gt; {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  app.get("/error", createErrorRoute(createHelpers))</span>
}

<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >import {</span>
  defaultConfig,
  getUrlForFlow,
  isQuerySet,
  logger,
  redirectOnSoftError,
  RouteCreator,
  RouteRegistrator,
} from "../pkg"
import { LoginFlow } from "@ory/client"
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >import { UserAuthCard } from "@ory/elements-markup"</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >import path from "path"</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >import { URLSearchParams } from "url"</span>
&nbsp;
export const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >createLoginRoute: RouteCreator =</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >  (c</span>reateHelpers) =&gt; <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >async (r</span></span>eq, res, next<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >) =&gt; {</span></span></span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    res.locals.projectName = "Sign in"</span>
&nbsp;
    const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >{</span>
      flow,
      aal = "",
      refresh = "",
      return_to = "",
      organization = "",
      via = "",
      login_challenge,
      identity_schema,
    } = req.query
    const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >{ frontend, kratosBrowserUrl, logoUrl, extraPartials } =</span>
      createHelpers(req, res)
&nbsp;
    const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >initFlowQuery = new URLSearchParams({</span>
      aal: aal.toString(),
      refresh: refresh.toString(),
      return_to: return_to.toString(),
      organization: organization.toString(),
      via: via.toString(),
    })
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    if (isQuerySet(login_challenge)) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      logger.debug("login_challenge found in URL query: ", { query: req.query })</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      initFlowQuery.append("login_challenge", login_challenge)</span>
    }
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    if (isQuerySet(identity_schema)) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      initFlowQuery.append("identity_schema", identity_schema)</span>
    }
&nbsp;
    const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >initFlowUrl = getUrlForFlow(kratosBrowserUrl, "login", initFlowQuery)</span>
&nbsp;
    // The flow is used to identify the settings and registration flow and
    // return data like the csrf_token and so on.
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    if (!isQuerySet(flow)) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      logger.debug("No flow ID found in URL query initializing login flow", {</span>
        query: req.query,
      })
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      res.redirect(303, initFlowUrl)</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      return</span>
    }
&nbsp;
    // It is probably a bit strange to have a logout URL here, however this screen
    // is also used for 2FA flows. If something goes wrong there, we probably want
    // to give the user the option to sign out!
    const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >getLogoutUrl = <span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >async (l</span>oginFlow: LoginFlow<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >) =&gt; {</span></span></span>
      let <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >logoutUrl = ""</span>
      try {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        logoutUrl = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >await frontend</span></span>
          .createBrowserLogoutFlow({
            cookie: req.header("cookie"),
            returnTo:
              (return_to &amp;&amp; return_to.toString()) || loginFlow.return_to || "",
          })
          .then(<span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >({</span> data<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" > })</span> =&gt; <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >data.logout_url)</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        return logoutUrl</span>
      } catch (err) {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        logger.error("Unable to create logout URL", { error: err })</span>
      }
    }
&nbsp;
    const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >redirectToVerificationFlow = <span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >(l</span>oginFlow: LoginFlow) =&gt; {</span>
      // we will create a new verification flow and redirect the user to the verification page
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      frontend</span>
        .createBrowserVerificationFlow({
          returnTo:
            (return_to &amp;&amp; return_to.toString()) || loginFlow.return_to || "",
        })
        .then(<span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >({</span> headers<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >, data: v</span>erificationFlow<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" > })</span> =&gt; {
          // we need the csrf cookie from the verification flow
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >          if (headers["set-cookie"]) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >            res.setHeader("set-cookie", headers["set-cookie"])</span>
          }
          // encode the verification flow id in the query parameters
          const verificationParameters = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >new URLSearchParams({</span>
            flow: verificationFlow.id,
            message: JSON.stringify(loginFlow.ui.messages),
          })
&nbsp;
          const baseUrl = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >req.path.split("/")</span>
          // get rid of the last part of the path (e.g. "login")
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >          baseUrl.pop()</span>
&nbsp;
          // redirect to the verification page with the custom message
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >          res.redirect(</span>
            303,
            // join the base url with the verification path
            path.join(
              req.baseUrl,
              "verification?" + verificationParameters.toString(),
            ),
          )
        })
        .catch(
          redirectOnSoftError(
            res,
            next,
            getUrlForFlow(
              kratosBrowserUrl,
              "verification",
              new URLSearchParams({
                return_to:
                  (return_to &amp;&amp; return_to.toString()) ||
                  loginFlow.return_to ||
                  "",
                ...(loginFlow.identity_schema &amp;&amp; {
                  identity_schema: loginFlow.identity_schema,
                }),
              }),
            ),
          ),
        )
    }
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    return frontend</span>
      .getLoginFlow({ id: flow, cookie: req.header("cookie") })
      .then(<span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >async ({</span> data: flow<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" > })</span> =&gt; {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        if (flow.ui.messages &amp;&amp; flow.ui.messages.length &gt; 0) {</span>
          // the login requires that the user verifies their email address before logging in
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >          if (flow.ui.messages.some(<span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >({</span> id<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" > })</span> =&gt; <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >id === 4000010))</span> {</span>
            // we will create a new verification flow and redirect the user to the verification page
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >            return redirectToVerificationFlow(flow)</span>
          }
        }
&nbsp;
        // Render the data using a view (e.g. Jade Template):
        const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >initRegistrationQuery = new URLSearchParams({</span>
          return_to:
            (return_to &amp;&amp; return_to.toString()) || flow.return_to || "",
          ...(flow.identity_schema &amp;&amp; {
            identity_schema: flow.identity_schema.toString(),
          }),
          ...(flow.oauth2_login_request?.challenge &amp;&amp; {
            login_challenge: flow.oauth2_login_request.challenge,
          }),
        })
&nbsp;
        let <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >initRecoveryUrl = ""</span>
        const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >initRegistrationUrl = getUrlForFlow(</span>
          kratosBrowserUrl,
          "registration",
          initRegistrationQuery,
        )
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        if (!flow.refresh) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >          initRecoveryUrl = getUrlForFlow(</span>
            kratosBrowserUrl,
            "recovery",
            new URLSearchParams({
              return_to:
                (return_to &amp;&amp; return_to.toString()) || flow.return_to || "",
            }),
          )
        }
&nbsp;
        let <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >logoutUrl: string | undefined = ""</span>
        if (<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >flow.</span>requested_aal === "aal2" || flow.refresh) {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >          logoutUrl = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >await getLogoutUrl(flow)</span></span>
        }
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        res.render("login", {</span>
          nodes: flow.ui.nodes,
          card: UserAuthCard(
            {
              flow,
              flowType: "login",
              cardImage: logoUrl,
              additionalProps: {
                forgotPasswordURL: initRecoveryUrl,
                signupURL: initRegistrationUrl,
                logoutURL: logoutUrl,
                loginURL: initFlowUrl,
              },
            },
            { locale: res.locals.lang },
          ),
          extraPartial: extraPartials?.login,
          extraContext: res.locals.extraContext,
        })
      })
      .catch(redirectOnSoftError(res, next, initFlowUrl))
  }
&nbsp;
export const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >registerLoginRoute: RouteRegistrator = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >(</span></span></span>
  app,
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  createHelpers = defaultConfig,</span>
) =&gt; {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  app.get("/login", createLoginRoute(createHelpers))</span>
}

<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >import {</span>
  RouteCreator,
  RouteRegistrator,
  defaultConfig,
  logger,
  setSession,
} from "../pkg"
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >import { UserLogoutCard } from "@ory/elements-markup"</span>
import { Request, Response, NextFunction } from "express"
&nbsp;
export const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >createShowLogoutRoute: RouteCreator =</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >  (c</span>reateHelpers) =&gt;</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >  async (r</span>eq: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >) =&gt; {</span></span></span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    res.locals.projectName = "Logout"</span>
&nbsp;
    const { logout_challenge: <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >logoutChallenge } = r</span>eq.query
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    if (typeof logoutChallenge !== "string") {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      logger.debug("Expected a logout challenge to be set but received none.")</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      res.redirect("login")</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      return</span>
    }
&nbsp;
    const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >{ oauth2, shouldSkipLogoutConsent } = c</span>reateHelpers(req, res)
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    oauth2</span>
      .getOAuth2LogoutRequest({ logoutChallenge })
      .then(<span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >({</span> data: body<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" > })</span> =&gt; {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        if (shouldSkipLogoutConsent(body)) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >          return oauth2</span>
            .acceptOAuth2LogoutRequest({ logoutChallenge })
            .then(<span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >({</span> data: body<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" > })</span> =&gt; <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >res.redirect(body.redirect_to))</span>
        }
&nbsp;
        // this should never happen
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        if (!req.csrfToken) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >          logger.warn(</span>
            "Expected CSRF token middleware to be set but received none.",
          )
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >          next(</span>
            new Error(
              "Expected CSRF token middleware to be set but received none.",
            ),
          )
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >          return</span>
        }
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        res.render("logout", {</span>
          card: UserLogoutCard({
            csrfToken: req.csrfToken(true),
            challenge: logoutChallenge,
            action: "logout",
          }),
        })
      })
      .catch(<span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >() =&gt; <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >r</span>es.redirect("login"))</span>
  }
&nbsp;
export const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >createSubmitLogoutRoute: RouteCreator =</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >  (c</span>reateHelpers) =&gt;</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >  async (r</span>eq: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >) =&gt; {</span></span></span>
    const { <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >oauth2 } = c</span>reateHelpers(req, res)
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    res.locals.projectName = "Logout"</span>
&nbsp;
    // The challenge is now a hidden input field, so let's take it from
    // the request body instead.
    const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >{ challenge: logoutChallenge, submit } = r</span>eq.body
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    if (submit === "No") {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      logger.debug("User rejected to log out.")</span>
      // The user rejected to log out, so we'll redirect to /ui/welcome
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      return oauth2</span>
        .rejectOAuth2LogoutRequest({ logoutChallenge })
        .then(<span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >() =&gt; <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >r</span>es.redirect("login"))</span>
        .catch(<span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >() =&gt; <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >r</span>es.redirect("login"))</span>
    } else {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      logger.debug("User agreed to log out.")</span>
      // The user agreed to log out, let's accept the logout request.
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      return oauth2</span>
        .acceptOAuth2LogoutRequest({ logoutChallenge })
        .then(<span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >({</span> data: body<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" > })</span> =&gt; <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >res.redirect(body.redirect_to))</span>
        .catch(<span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >() =&gt; <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >r</span>es.redirect("login"))</span>
    }
  }
&nbsp;
export const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >registerLogoutRoute: RouteRegistrator = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >(</span></span></span>
  app,
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  createHelpers = defaultConfig,</span>
) =&gt; {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  app.get(</span>
    "/logout",
    setSession(createHelpers),
    createShowLogoutRoute(createHelpers),
  )
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  app.post(</span>
    "/logout",
    setSession(createHelpers),
    createSubmitLogoutRoute(createHelpers),
  )
}

<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >  (c</span>reateHelpers) =&gt; <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >(r</span></span>eq, res, next) =&gt; {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    res.locals.projectName = "Recover account"</span>
&nbsp;
    const { flow<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >, <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >r</span>eturn_to<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" > </span>= "" } = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >r</span>eq.query</span>
    const { frontend<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >, k</span>ratosBrowserUrl<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >, l</span>ogoUrl<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >, f</span>aviconUrl<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >, f</span>aviconType<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" > } =</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      createHelpers(req, res)</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    res.locals.faviconUrl = faviconUrl</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    res.locals.faviconType = faviconType</span>
    const initFlowUrl = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >getUrlForFlow(</span>
      kratosBrowserUrl,
      "recovery",
      new URLSearchParams({ return_to: return_to.toString() }),
    )
&nbsp;
    // The flow is used to identify the settings and registration flow and
    // return data like the csrf_token and so on.
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    if (!isQuerySet(flow)) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      logger.debug("No flow ID found in URL query initializing login flow", {</span>
        query: req.query,
      })
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      res.redirect(303, initFlowUrl)</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      return</span>
    }
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    return frontend</span>
      .getRecoveryFlow({ id: flow, cookie: req.header("cookie") })
      .then(<span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >({</span> data: flow<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" > })</span> =&gt; {
        const initLoginUrl = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >getUrlForFlow(</span>
          kratosBrowserUrl,
          "login",
          new URLSearchParams({
            return_to:
              (return_to &amp;&amp; return_to.toString()) || flow.return_to || "",
          }),
        )
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        res.render("recovery", {</span>
          card: UserAuthCard(
            {
              flow,
              flowType: "recovery",
              cardImage: logoUrl,
              additionalProps: {
                loginURL: initLoginUrl,
              },
            },
            {
              locale: res.locals.lang,
            },
          ),
        })
      })
      .catch(redirectOnSoftError(res, next, initFlowUrl))
  }
&nbsp;

<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >  (c</span>reateHelpers) =&gt; <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >async (r</span></span>eq, res, next<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >) =&gt; {</span></span></span>

&nbsp;
      // :two: Try to fetch session (exists only AFTER successful registration)
      const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >sessionResponse = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >await frontend</span></span>
        .toSession({ cookie: req.header("Cookie") })
        .catch(<span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >() =&gt; <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >n</span>ull)</span>
&nbsp;
      if (<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >sessionResponse?</span>.data?.identity) {
        const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >identity = sessionResponse.data.identity</span>
        const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >userId = identity.id</span>
        const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >tenantId = identity.traits?.tenant_id?.[0]</span>
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        if (!tenantId) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >          throw new Error("tenant_id missing in identity traits")</span>
        }
&nbsp;
        // :three: CALL KETO BOOTSTRAP :white_check_mark:
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        await axios.post(`${KETO_BOOTSTRAP_URL}/bootstrap/tenant`, {</span>
          tenantId,
          userId,
        })
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        logger.info(":white_check_mark: Keto tuple created automatically", {</span>
          tenantId,
          userId,
        })
&nbsp;
        // :four: Redirect after successful registration
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        return res.redirect(303, "/welcome")</span>
      }
&nbsp;
      // :five: Render registration UI (before submit)
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      res.render("registration", {</span>
        nodes: flowData.ui.nodes,
        card: UserAuthCard(
          {
            flow: flowData,
            flowType: "registration",
            cardImage: logoUrl,
          },
          { locale: res.locals.lang },
        ),
        extraPartial: extraPartials?.registration,
        extraContext: res.locals.extraContext,
      })
    } catch (err) {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      redirectOnSoftError(res, next, initFlowUrl)(err as any)</span>
    }
  }
&nbsp;
export const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >registerRegistrationRoute: RouteRegistrator = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >(</span></span></span>
  app,
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  createHelpers = defaultConfig,</span>
) =&gt; {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  app.get("/registration", createRegistrationRoute(createHelpers))</span>
}</pre></td></tr></table></pre>

import { RouteRegistrator } from "../pkg";
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >import fetch from "node-fetch";</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >import { createJwt } from "../services/jwt.service";</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >import { getUserTenants } from "../services/tenantAccess.service";</span>
&nbsp;
export const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >registerSessionExportRoute: RouteRegistrator = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >(</span>a</span>pp) =&gt; {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  app.post("/api/session/jwt", <span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >async (r</span>eq: Request, res: Response<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >) =&gt; {</span></span></span>
    try {
      const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >sessionCookie = req.cookies["ory_kratos_session"];</span>
      const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >sessionToken = req.headers["x-session-token"] as string | undefined;</span>
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      if (!sessionCookie &amp;&amp; !sessionToken) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        return res.status(401).json({</span>
          error: "No Kratos session found. Please login first.",
        });
      }
&nbsp;
      // ðŸ” Call Kratos whoami
      const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >whoamiRes = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >await fetch(</span></span>
        `${process.env.KRATOS_PUBLIC_URL}/sessions/whoami`,
        {
          headers: sessionToken
            ? { "X-Session-Token": sessionToken }
            : { Cookie: `ory_kratos_session=${sessionCookie}` },
        }
      );
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      if (!whoamiRes.ok) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        return res.status(401).json({ error: "Invalid Kratos session" });</span>
      }
&nbsp;
      const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >whoami: any = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >await whoamiRes.json();</span></span>
&nbsp;
      const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >identityId = whoami.identity.id;</span>
      const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >email = whoami.identity.traits.email;</span>
      const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >roles = whoami.identity.traits.roles || [];</span>
&nbsp;
      // ðŸ” Only admins can get admin JWT
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      if (!roles.includes("platform.admin")) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >        return res.status(403).json({</span>
          error: "Only admin can obtain admin JWT",
        });
      }
&nbsp;
      // ðŸ“¦ Fetch tenant permissions from DB
      const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >tenants = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >await getUserTenants(identityId);</span></span>
&nbsp;
      // ðŸ”‘ CREATE JWT (THIS IS THE KEY PART)
      const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >token = createJwt({</span>
        sub: identityId,
        email,
        role: "platform.admin",
        tenants,
      });
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      return res.json({</span>
        access_token: token,
        expires_in: process.env.JWT_EXPIRES || "1h",
      });
    } catch (err) {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      console.error(err);</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      return res.status(500).json({ error: "Failed to create JWT" });</span>
    }
  });
};

import { requireJwt } from "../lib/requireJwt";
import { checkTenantAccess } from "../services/tenantAccess.service";
&nbsp;
const router = express.Router();
&nbsp;
router.post(
  "/api/tenant/access/check",
  express.json(),
  requireJwt,
  async (req: any, res) =&gt; {
    try {
      const identityId = req.jwt.sub;
      const { tenantId, action } = req.body;
&nbsp;
      if (!tenantId || !action) {
        return res.status(400).json({
          error: "tenantId and action required",
        });
      }
&nbsp;
      const allowed = await checkTenantAccess(
        tenantId,
        identityId,
        action
      );
&nbsp;
      res.json({ allowed });
    } catch {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      res.status(500).json({</span>
        error: "Keto access check failed",
      });
    }
  }
);
&nbsp;
export default router;
&nbsp;</pre></td></tr></table></pre>

export const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >createVerificationRoute: RouteCreator =</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >  (c</span>reateHelpers) =&gt; <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >(r</span></span>eq, res, next) =&gt; {</span>

&nbsp;

      kratosBrowserUrl,
      "verification",
      new URLSearchParams({ return_to: return_to.toString() }),
    )
&nbsp;
    // The flow is used to identify the settings and registration flow and
    // return data like the csrf_token and so on.
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    if (!isQuerySet(flow)) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      logger.debug("No flow ID found in URL query initializing login flow", {</span>
        query: req.query,
      })
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      res.redirect(303, initFlowUrl)</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      return</span>
    }
&nbsp;

      frontend

        .then(<span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >({</span> data: flow<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" > })</span> =&gt; {
          const initRegistrationUrl = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >getUrlForFlow(</span>
            kratosBrowserUrl,
            "registration",
            new URLSearchParams({
              return_to:

            }),
          )
&nbsp;
          // check for custom messages in the query string
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >          if (isQuerySet(message)) {</span>
            const m: UiText[] = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >JSON.parse(message)</span>
&nbsp;
            // add them to the flow data so they can be rendered by the UI
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >            flow.ui.messages = [...(flow.ui.messages || []), ...m]</span>

&nbsp;

                flow,
                flowType: "verification",

                },

              { locale: res.locals.lang },
            ),
          })
        })
        // Handle errors using ExpressJS' next functionality:
        .catch(redirectOnSoftError(res, next, initFlowUrl))
    )
  }

   OpenID Connect Conformance test suite. You can use it for inspiration, but please
   do not use it in production as is. */
import {
  OAuth2LoginRequest,
  AcceptOAuth2ConsentRequestSession,
} from "@ory/client"
&nbsp;
export const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >oidcConformityMaybeFakeAcr = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >(</span></span></span>
  request: OAuth2LoginRequest,
  fallback: string,
) =&gt; {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  if (process.env.CONFORMITY_FAKE_CLAIMS !== "1") {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    return fallback</span>
  }
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  return request.oidc_context?.acr_values &amp;&amp;</span>
    request.oidc_context.acr_values.length &gt; 0
    ? request.oidc_context.acr_values[
        request.oidc_context.acr_values.length - 1
      ]
    : fallback
}
&nbsp;
export const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >oidcConformityMaybeFakeSession = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >(</span></span></span>
  grantScope: string[],
  session: AcceptOAuth2ConsentRequestSession,
): AcceptOAuth2ConsentRequestSession =&gt; {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  if (process.env.CONFORMITY_FAKE_CLAIMS !== "1") {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    return session</span>
  }
&nbsp;
  const idToken: { [key: string]: any } = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >{}</span>
&nbsp;
  // If the email scope was granted, fake the email claims.
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  if (grantScope.indexOf("email") &gt; -1) {</span>
    // But only do so if the email was requested!
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    idToken.email = "foo@bar.com"</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    idToken.email_verified = true</span>
  }
&nbsp;

<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  if (grantScope.indexOf("phone") &gt; -1) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    idToken.phone_number = "1337133713371337"</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    idToken.phone_number_verified = true</span>
  }
&nbsp;
  // If the profile scope was granted, fake the profile claims.
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  if (grantScope.indexOf("profile") &gt; -1) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    idToken.name = "Foo Bar"</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    idToken.given_name = "Foo"</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    idToken.family_name = "Bar"</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    idToken.website = "https://www.ory.sh"</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    idToken.zoneinfo = "Europe/Belrin"</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    idToken.birthdate = "1.1.2014"</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    idToken.gender = "robot"</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    idToken.profile = "https://www.ory.sh"</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    idToken.preferred_username = "robot"</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    idToken.middle_name = "Baz"</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    idToken.locale = "en-US"</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    idToken.picture =</span>
      "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ory/web/master/static/images/favico.png"
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    idToken.updated_at = 1604416603</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    idToken.nickname = "foobot"</span>
  }

      ...idToken,
      ...session.id_token,
    },
  }

<span class="cline-any cline-neutral">&nbsp;</span></td><td class="text"><pre class="prettyprint lang-js">import fetch from "node-fetch";
&nbsp;
const KRATOS_ADMIN_URL =
  process.env.KRATOS_ADMIN_URL || <span class="branch-1 cbranch-no" title="branch not covered" >"http://localhost:4434";</span>
&nbsp;
export type KratosTraits = {
  email: string;
  tenant_id: string[];
  roles: string[];
};
&nbsp;
export type KratosIdentity = {
  id: string;
  traits: KratosTraits;
};
&nbsp;
type KratosCreateResponse = {
  id: string;
  traits: KratosTraits;
};
&nbsp;
type KratosUpdateResponse = {
  id: string;
  traits: KratosTraits;
};
&nbsp;
const SCHEMA_ID = "scansure_v1";
&nbsp;
&nbsp;
// =====================================================
// âœ… CREATE IDENTITY
// =====================================================
&nbsp;
export async function <span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >createKratosIdentity(</span>
  {
    email<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >,</span>
    tenantId<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >,</span>
    roles<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >,</span>
  }: {
    email: string;
    tenantId: string;
    roles: string[];
  }
): Promise&lt;KratosIdentity&gt; {
&nbsp;
  const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >payload = {</span>
    schema_id: SCHEMA_ID,
    traits: {
      email,
      tenant_id: [tenantId],
      roles,
    },
    credentials: {
      password: {
        config: {
          password: "Temp1234!",
        },
      },
    },
  };
&nbsp;
  const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >res = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >await fetch(`${KRATOS_ADMIN_URL}/admin/identities`, {</span></span>
    method: "POST",
    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    body: JSON.stringify(payload),
  });
&nbsp;
  const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >text = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >await res.text();</span></span>
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  if (!res.ok) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    console.error("âŒ Kratos CREATE status:", res.status);</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    console.error("âŒ Kratos CREATE response:", text);</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    throw new Error(text);</span>
  }
&nbsp;
  const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >data = JSON.parse(text) as KratosCreateResponse;</span>
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  if (!data?.id) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    throw new Error("Kratos returned no identity id");</span>
  }
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  console.log("âœ… Kratos identity created:", data.id);</span>
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  return data;</span>
}
&nbsp;
&nbsp;
&nbsp;
// =====================================================
// âœ… UPDATE IDENTITY
// =====================================================
&nbsp;
export async function <span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >updateKratosIdentity(</span>
  identityId: string,
  {
    email<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >,</span>
    tenantIds<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >,</span>
    roles<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >,</span>
    newPassword<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >,</span>
  }: {
    email: string;
    tenantIds: string[];
    roles: string[];
    newPassword?: string;
  }
): Promise&lt;KratosIdentity&gt; {
&nbsp;
  const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >payload: any = {</span>
    schema_id: SCHEMA_ID,
    traits: {
      email,
      tenant_id: tenantIds,
      roles,
    },
  };
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  if (newPassword) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    payload.credentials = {</span>
      password: {
        config: { password: newPassword },
      },
    };
  }
&nbsp;
  const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >res = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >await fetch(</span></span>
    `${KRATOS_ADMIN_URL}/admin/identities/${identityId}`,
    {
      method: "PUT",
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      body: JSON.stringify(payload),
    }
  );
&nbsp;
  const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >text = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >await res.text();</span></span>
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  if (!res.ok) {</span>
&nbsp;
    // âœ… HANDLE CONFLICT CLEANLY
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    if (res.status === 409) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      console.warn("âš ï¸ Kratos conflict â€” identity already has same values");</span>
      
      // return current identity instead of crashing
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >      return getKratosIdentity(identityId);</span>
    }
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    console.error("âŒ Kratos UPDATE status:", res.status);</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    console.error("âŒ Kratos UPDATE response:", text);</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    throw new Error(text);</span>
  }
&nbsp;
  const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >data = JSON.parse(text) as KratosUpdateResponse;</span>
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  console.log("âœ… Kratos identity updated:", data.id);</span>
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  return data;</span>
}
&nbsp;
// =====================================================
// âœ… LIST ALL IDENTITIES
// =====================================================

export async function <span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >listKratosIdentities(</span>): Promise&lt;KratosIdentity[]&gt; {
  const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >res = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="branch-0 cbranch-no" title="branch not covered" >await fetch(`${KRATOS_ADMIN_URL}/admin/identities`);</span></span></span>
&nbsp;
  const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >text = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >await res.text();</span></span>
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  if (!res.ok) {</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    console.error("âŒ Kratos LIST status:", res.status);</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    console.error("âŒ Kratos LIST response:", text);</span>
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    throw new Error(text);</span>
  }
&nbsp;
  const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >data = JSON.parse(text);</span>
&nbsp;

&nbsp;

}

<span class="cline-any cline-neutral">&nbsp;</span></td><td class="text"><pre class="prettyprint lang-js">import axios from "axios";
import { pool } from "../lib/db"
import { query } from "../lib/db";
&nbsp;
const KETO_ADMIN_URL =
  process.env.KETO_ADMIN_URL || "http://localhost:4467";
&nbsp;
const KETO_READ_URL =
  process.env.KETO_READ_URL || "http://localhost:4466";
&nbsp;
export type UserTenant = {
  tenantId: string;
  tenantName: string;
  role: string;
};
&nbsp;
//
// âœ… DB â€” list user tenants
//
export async function <span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >getUserTenants(</span>
  identityId: string
): Promise&lt;UserTenant[]&gt; {
  const <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >result = <span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" ><span class="branch-0 cbranch-no" title="branch not covered" >await query(</span></span></span>
    `
    SELECT
      t.id   AS "tenantId",
      t.name AS "tenantName",
      tu.role
    FROM tenant_users tu
    JOIN tenants t ON t.id = tu.tenant_id
    WHERE tu.identity_id = $1
    ORDER BY t.created_at
    `,
    [identityId]
  );
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >  return result.rows;</span>
}
&nbsp;
//
// âœ… Keto â€” write relation tuple
//
export async function <span class="fstat-no" title="function not covered" >addUserToTenantRelation(</span>
  tenantId: string,
  identityId: string,
  role: string
) {
  try {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    await axios.put(</span>
      `${KETO_ADMIN_URL}/admin/relation-tuples`,
      {
        namespace: "tenant",
        object: tenantId,
        relation: role,
        subject_id: identityId,   // correct for Keto v0.11
      },
      {
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      }
    );
&nbsp;
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    console.log("âœ… Keto relation created");</span>
  } catch (err: any) {
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    console.error(</span>
      "Keto relation failed:",
      err.response?.data || err.message
    );
<span class="cstat-no" title="statement not covered" >    throw err;</span>
  }
}
&nbsp;
//
// âœ… Keto â€” access check (READ API)
//
export async function checkTenantAccess(
  tenantId: string,
  subject: string,
  role: string
): Promise&lt;boolean&gt; {
  const result = await pool.query(
    `
      select 1
      from tenant_access
      where tenant_id = $1
        and subject = $2
        and role = $3
      limit 1
    `,
    [tenantId, subject, role]
  )
&nbsp;
  return result.rows.length &gt; 0
}

<span class="cline-any cline-neutral">&nbsp;</span>

<span class="cline-any cline-neutral">&nbsp;</span>
<span class="cline-any cline-neutral">&nbsp;</span>
<span class="cline-any cline-neutral">&nbsp;</span>
<span class="cline-any cline-neutral">&nbsp;</span>
<span class="cline-any cline-neutral">&nbsp;</span>
<span class="cline-any cline-yes">4x</span>
<span class="cline-any cline-neutral">&nbsp;</span>
<span class="cline-any cline-neutral">&nbsp;</span>
<span class="cline-any cline-yes">3x</span>
<span class="cline-any cline-neutral">&nbsp;</span>
<span class="cline-any cline-neutral">&nbsp;</span>
<span class="cline-any cline-neutral">&nbsp;</span></td><td class="text"><pre class="prettyprint lang-js">import { withTransaction } from "../lib/db";
import { addUserToTenantRelation } from "./tenantAccess.service";
&nbsp;
export type TenantRow = {
  id: string;
  name: string;
};
&nbsp;
export async function createMultipleTenants(
  tenantNames: string[],
  identityId: string
): Promise&lt;TenantRow[]&gt; {
  return withTransaction(async (client) =&gt; {
    const tenants: TenantRow[] = [];
&nbsp;
    for (const name of tenantNames) {
      // 1ï¸âƒ£ Create tenant (idempotent)
      const insert = await client.query(
        `
        INSERT INTO tenants (name)
        VALUES ($1)
        ON CONFLICT (name) DO NOTHING
        RETURNING id, name
        `,
        [name]
      );
&nbsp;
      // 2ï¸âƒ£ Get tenant row (new or existing)
      const tenant: TenantRow =
        insert.rows[0] ??
        (
          await client.query(
            `SELECT id, name FROM tenants WHERE name = $1`,
            [name]
          )
        ).rows[0];
&nbsp;
      // 3ï¸âƒ£ Ensure DB mapping (idempotent)
      await client.query(
        `
        INSERT INTO tenant_users (tenant_id, identity_id, role)
        VALUES ($1, $2, 'platform.admin')
        ON CONFLICT (tenant_id, identity_id) DO NOTHING
        `,
        [tenant.id, identityId]
      );
&nbsp;
      // 4ï¸âƒ£ Ensure Keto relation (idempotent by design)
      await addUserToTenantRelation(
        tenant.id,
        identityId,
        "platform.admin"
      );
&nbsp;
      tenants.push(tenant);
    }
&nbsp;
    return tenants;
  });
}
&nbsp;</pre></td></tr></table></pre>

### Folder: C:\Users\Kasetti User\Desktop\kratos-selfservice-ui-node\infra\auth

===== auth.js =====
const jwt = require("jsonwebtoken");
const axios = require("axios");

const KETO_READ_URL = process.env.KETO_READ_URL || "http://keto:4466";

module.exports = async function auth(req, res, next) {
  const header = req.headers.authorization;

  if (!header) {
    return res.status(401).json({ msg: "JWT required" });
  }

  const token = header.replace("Bearer ", "");

  try {
    const decoded = jwt.verify(token, process.env.JWT_SECRET);
    const userId = decoded.userId;
    const roles = decoded.roles || [];

    let requestedTenants = req.query.tenant;
    if (!requestedTenants) {
      return res.status(400).json({ msg: "tenant query param required" });
    }

    if (!Array.isArray(requestedTenants)) {
      requestedTenants = [requestedTenants];
    }

    // ðŸ”¥ LIVE CHECK WITH KETO
    for (const tenant of requestedTenants) {
      const { data } = await axios.get(
        `${KETO_READ_URL}/relation-tuples/check`,
        {
          params: {
            namespace: "tenant",
            object: tenant,
            relation: "access",
            subject_id: userId
          }
        }
      );

      if (!data.allowed) {
        return res.status(403).json({
          msg: "Access denied for tenant",
          tenant
        });
      }
    }

    req.userId = userId;
    req.tenants = requestedTenants;
    req.roles = roles;

    next();

  } catch (err) {
    console.error("AUTH ERROR:", err.response?.data || err.message);
    res.status(401).json({ msg: "Authorization service error" });
  }
};

### Folder: C:\Users\Kasetti User\Desktop\kratos-selfservice-ui-node\infra\auth

===== server.js =====
require("dotenv").config();
const express = require("express");
const axios = require("axios");
const jwt = require("jsonwebtoken");

const auth = require("./auth");

const app = express();
app.use(express.json());

const KRATOS_PUBLIC_URL = process.env.KRATOS_PUBLIC_URL;

/**
 * STEP 1: Exchange SESSION TOKEN â†’ JWT
 */
app.get("/jwt", async (req, res) => {
  const sessionToken = req.headers["x-session-token"];

  if (!sessionToken) {
    return res.status(401).json({ msg: "Session token required" });
  }

  try {
    const { data } = await axios.get(
      `${process.env.KRATOS_PUBLIC_URL}/sessions/whoami`,
      {
        headers: {
          "X-Session-Token": sessionToken
        }
      }
    );

    const userId = data.identity.id;
    const roles = data.identity.traits.roles || [];

    const token = jwt.sign(
      { userId, roles },
      process.env.JWT_SECRET,
      { expiresIn: "1h" }
    );

    res.json({ token });

  } catch (err) {
    console.error(err.response?.data || err.message);
    res.status(401).json({ msg: "Invalid session token" });
  }
});

/**
 * STEP 2: Protected API
 */
app.get("/api/data", auth, (req, res) => {
  res.json({
    msg: "Access granted",
    user: req.userId,
    tenants: req.tenants,
    roles: req.roles
  });
});

app.listen(process.env.PORT, () => {
  console.log(`âœ… Auth service running on port ${process.env.PORT}`);
});
